verses.hebrew_full: דְּבַר־יְהֹוָה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר הָיָ֔ה אֶל־יוֹאֵ֖ל בֶּן־פְּתוּאֵֽל
verses.translation: The word of GOD that came to Joel son of Pethuel.
verses.rashi: to Joel son of Pethuel—The son of Samuel the prophet who persuaded God with his prayer (פִתָּה לְאֵל) . Some say that this prophecy was said in those seven years in which Elisha said: “For the Lord has decreed a famine etc.” and they took place during the days of Jehoram son of Ahab.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁמְעוּ־זֹאת֙ הַזְּקֵנִ֔ים וְהַֽאֲזִ֔ינוּ כֹּ֖ל יוֹשְׁבֵ֣י הָאָ֑רֶץ הֶהָ֤יְתָה זֹּאת֙ בִּֽימֵיכֶ֔ם וְאִ֖ם בִּימֵ֥י אֲבֹתֵיכֶֽם
verses.translation: Listen to this, O elders,Give ear, all inhabitants of the land.Has the like of this happened in your daysOr in the days of your ancestors?
verses.rashi: Did this come about—I.e., what is mentioned below.

verses.hebrew_full: עָלֶ֖יהָ לִבְנֵיכֶ֣ם סַפֵּ֑רוּ וּבְנֵיכֶם֙ לִבְנֵיהֶ֔ם וּבְנֵיהֶ֖ם לְד֥וֹר אַחֵֽר
verses.translation: Tell your children about it,And let your children tell theirs,And their children the next generation!

verses.hebrew_full: יֶ֤תֶר הַגָּזָם֙ אָכַ֣ל הָאַרְבֶּ֔ה וְיֶ֥תֶר הָאַרְבֶּ֖ה אָכַ֣ל הַיָּ֑לֶק וְיֶ֣תֶר הַיֶּ֔לֶק אָכַ֖ל הֶחָסִֽיל
verses.translation: What the cutter has left, the locust has devoured;What the locust has left, the grub has devoured;And what the grub has left, the hopper has devoured.
verses.rashi: What the shearing locust left over—Gazam, arbeh, yelek, hasil are species of locusts and he prophesied concerning them that they would come in those days and destroy all the fruit of the trees and the herbs of the field.

verses.hebrew_full: הָקִ֤יצוּ שִׁכּוֹרִים֙ וּבְכ֔וּ וְהֵילִ֖לוּ כׇּל־שֹׁ֣תֵי יָ֑יִן עַל־עָסִ֕יס כִּ֥י נִכְרַ֖ת מִפִּיכֶֽם
verses.translation: Wake up, you drunkards, and weep,Wail, all you swillers of wine—For the new wine that is denied you!
verses.rashi: the strong wine—Heb. עָסִיס, good wine.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־גוֹי֙ עָלָ֣ה עַל־אַרְצִ֔י עָצ֖וּם וְאֵ֣ין מִסְפָּ֑ר שִׁנָּיו֙ שִׁנֵּ֣י אַרְיֵ֔ה וּֽמְתַלְּע֥וֹת לָבִ֖יא לֽוֹ
verses.translation: For a nation has invaded my land,Vast beyond counting,With teeth like the teeth of a lion,With the fangs of a lion’s breed.
verses.rashi: For a nation has ascended upon my land—He named these locusts as a host of nations. and its molars are like those of a young lion—These are the thick teeth with which he chews.

verses.hebrew_full: שָׂ֤ם גַּפְנִי֙ לְשַׁמָּ֔ה וּתְאֵנָתִ֖י לִקְצָפָ֑ה חָשֹׂ֤ף חֲשָׂפָהּ֙ וְהִשְׁלִ֔יךְ הִלְבִּ֖ינוּ שָׂרִיגֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: They have laid my vines wasteAnd splintered my fig trees:They have stripped off their bark and thrown [it] away;Their runners have turned white.
verses.rashi: into a disappointment—Heb. לִקְצָפָה, into a disappointment. he has peeled it and cast it off—He has peeled off the bark of the tree and cast it off until the branches of the vine have become white.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱלִ֕י כִּבְתוּלָ֥ה חֲגֻרַת־שַׂ֖ק עַל־בַּ֥עַל נְעוּרֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: Lament—like a maiden girt with sackclothFor the husband of her youth!
verses.rashi: Lament Heb. אֱלִי. Lamentations (קִינָה) is translated into Aramic as אִילְיָא.

verses.hebrew_full: הׇכְרַ֥ת מִנְחָ֛ה וָנֶ֖סֶךְ מִבֵּ֣ית יְהֹוָ֑ה אָֽבְלוּ֙ הַכֹּ֣הֲנִ֔ים מְשָׁרְתֵ֖י יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: Offering and libation have ceasedFrom the House of GOD;The priests must mournWho minister to GOD.
verses.rashi: have been cut off Heb. הָכְרַת, like נִכְרַת. The “hey” vowelized with a short “kamatz” takes the place of a “nun.” Comp. (Job 5:23), “And the beast of the field made peace (הָשְׁלְמָה) with you.”

verses.hebrew_full: שֻׁדַּ֣ד שָׂדֶ֔ה אָבְלָ֖ה אֲדָמָ֑ה כִּ֚י שֻׁדַּ֣ד דָּגָ֔ן הוֹבִ֥ישׁ תִּיר֖וֹשׁ אֻמְלַ֥ל יִצְהָֽר
verses.translation: The country is ravaged,The ground must mourn;For the new grain is ravaged,The new wine is dried up,The new oil has failed.

verses.hebrew_full: הֹבִ֣ישׁוּ אִכָּרִ֗ים הֵילִ֙ילוּ֙ כֹּֽרְמִ֔ים עַל־חִטָּ֖ה וְעַל־שְׂעֹרָ֑ה כִּ֥י אָבַ֖ד קְצִ֥יר שָׂדֶֽה
verses.translation: Farmers are dismayedAnd vine dressers wailOver wheat and barley;For the crops of the field are lost.
verses.rashi: Be ashamed Heb. הֹבִישוּ plowmen Heb. אִכָּרִים. Those who guide the plowshare. for wheat and for barley—This refers back to the plowmen, and concerning the vine dressers, he says...

verses.hebrew_full: הַגֶּ֣פֶן הוֹבִ֔ישָׁה וְהַתְּאֵנָ֖ה אֻמְלָ֑לָה רִמּ֞וֹן גַּם־תָּמָ֣ר וְתַפּ֗וּחַ כׇּל־עֲצֵ֤י הַשָּׂדֶה֙ יָבֵ֔שׁוּ כִּי־הֹבִ֥ישׁ שָׂשׂ֖וֹן מִן־בְּנֵ֥י אָדָֽם
verses.translation: The vine has dried up,The fig tree withers,Pomegranate, palm, and apple—All the trees of the field are sear.And joy has dried upAmong the people.
verses.rashi: The vines have dried up, and the fig trees have been cut off Heb. הוֹבִישָׁה, it was cut off, its produce terminated. has dried up Heb. הֹבִיש, like יָבֵש

verses.hebrew_full: חִגְר֨וּ וְסִפְד֜וּ הַכֹּהֲנִ֗ים הֵילִ֙ילוּ֙ מְשָׁרְתֵ֣י מִזְבֵּ֔חַ בֹּ֚אוּ לִ֣ינוּ בַשַּׂקִּ֔ים מְשָׁרְתֵ֖י אֱלֹהָ֑י כִּ֥י נִמְנַ֛ע מִבֵּ֥ית אֱלֹהֵיכֶ֖ם מִנְחָ֥ה וָנָֽסֶךְ
verses.translation: Gird yourselves and lament, O priests,Wail, O ministers of the altar;Come, spend the night in sackcloth,O ministers of my God.For offering and libation are withheldFrom the House of your God.

verses.hebrew_full: קַדְּשׁוּ־צוֹם֙ קִרְא֣וּ עֲצָרָ֔ה אִסְפ֣וּ זְקֵנִ֗ים כֹּ֚ל יֹשְׁבֵ֣י הָאָ֔רֶץ בֵּ֖ית יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֵיכֶ֑ם וְזַעֲק֖וּ אֶל־יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: Solemnize a fast,Proclaim an assembly;Gather the elders—all the inhabitants of the land—In the House of the ETERNAL your God,And cry out to GOD.
verses.rashi: Proclaim a fast Heb. קַדְּשׁוּ, designate a fast an assembly Heb. עֲצָרָה, a gathering.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲהָ֖הּ לַיּ֑וֹם כִּ֤י קָרוֹב֙ י֣וֹם יְהֹוָ֔ה וּכְשֹׁ֖ד מִשַּׁדַּ֥י יָבֽוֹא
verses.translation: Alas for the day!For the day of GOD is near;It shall come like havoc from Shaddai.
verses.rashi: Woe Heb. אֲהָהּ. An expression of sighing and wailing. and like plunder Heb. וּכְשֹׁד. Like the plunder of brigands. it will come—from the Omnipresent.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲל֛וֹא נֶ֥גֶד עֵינֵ֖ינוּ אֹ֣כֶל נִכְרָ֑ת מִבֵּ֥ית אֱלֹהֵ֖ינוּ שִׂמְחָ֥ה וָגִֽיל
verses.translation: For food is cut offBefore our very eyes,And joy and gladnessFrom the House of our God.
verses.rashi: from before our eyes—I.e., we see it.

verses.hebrew_full: עָבְשׁ֣וּ פְרֻד֗וֹת תַּ֚חַת מֶגְרְפֹ֣תֵיהֶ֔ם נָשַׁ֙מּוּ֙ אֹֽצָר֔וֹת נֶהֶרְס֖וּ מַמְּגֻר֑וֹת כִּ֥י הֹבִ֖ישׁ דָּגָֽן
verses.translation: The seeds have shriveledUnder their clods. The granaries are desolate,Barns are in ruins,For the new grain has failed.
verses.rashi: have gathered mold Heb. עָבְשׁוּ, have gathered rust and mold. in French moisir, to become moldy. casks of wine Heb. פְרֻדּדוֹת. Jonathan renders: casks of wine. under their bungs Heb. מֶגְרְפֹתֵיהֶם. Jonathan renders: מְגוּפָתֵיהוֹן, the cover of the cask. the storehouses are laid desolate—I.e. the storehouses of wine and oil. garners are demolished—Granaries of wheat.

verses.hebrew_full: מַה־נֶּאֶנְחָ֣ה בְהֵמָ֗ה נָבֹ֙כוּ֙ עֶדְרֵ֣י בָקָ֔ר כִּ֛י אֵ֥ין מִרְעֶ֖ה לָהֶ֑ם גַּם־עֶדְרֵ֥י הַצֹּ֖אן נֶאְשָֽׁמוּ
verses.translation: How the animals groan!The herds of cattle are bewilderedBecause they have no pasture,And the flocks of sheep are dazed.
verses.rashi: herds of cattle are perplexed—They are confounded. They are closed in and astray in the forests and in the wilderness. are laid waste Heb. נֶאְשָׁמוּ. have become spoiled through their guilt.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵלֶ֥יךָ יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶקְרָ֑א כִּ֣י אֵ֗שׁ אָֽכְלָה֙ נְא֣וֹת מִדְבָּ֔ר וְלֶ֣הָבָ֔ה לִהֲטָ֖ה כׇּל־עֲצֵ֥י הַשָּׂדֶֽה
verses.translation: To You, O ETERNAL One, I call.For fire has consumedThe pastures in the wilderness,And flame has devouredAll the trees of the countryside.
verses.rashi: the dwellings of the wilderness Heb. נְאוֹת, an expression of a dwelling (נָוֶה).

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־בַּהֲמ֥וֹת שָׂדֶ֖ה תַּעֲר֣וֹג אֵלֶ֑יךָ כִּ֤י יָֽבְשׁוּ֙ אֲפִ֣יקֵי מָ֔יִם וְאֵ֕שׁ אָכְלָ֖ה נְא֥וֹת הַמִּדְבָּֽר
verses.translation: The very beasts of the fieldCry out to You;For the watercourses are dried up,And fire has consumedThe pastures in the wilderness.
verses.rashi: cry out Heb. תַּעֲרֹג, will cry out, as Dunash (Teshuvoth Dunash, p. 18) interpreted it: ערג is the cry of the hinds. נהם is the roar of the lions; געה is the lowing of the calves; צהל is the whinnying of the horses; צִפצוּף is the chirping of the birds, and נבח is the barking of the dogs. [Also the beasts of the field—I.e., the hind of the field.]

verses.hebrew_full: תִּקְע֨וּ שׁוֹפָ֜ר בְּצִיּ֗וֹן וְהָרִ֙יעוּ֙ בְּהַ֣ר קׇדְשִׁ֔י יִרְגְּז֕וּ כֹּ֖ל יֹשְׁבֵ֣י הָאָ֑רֶץ כִּי־בָ֥א יוֹם־יְהֹוָ֖ה כִּ֥י קָרֽוֹב
verses.translation: Blow a horn in Zion,Sound an alarm on My holy mount!Let all dwellers on earth tremble,For the day of GOD has come!It is close—
verses.rashi: Sound a shophar—to announce to them that they should repent before the evil befalls them. the day of the Lord—which we mentioned above.

verses.hebrew_full: י֧וֹם חֹ֣שֶׁךְ וַאֲפֵלָ֗ה י֤וֹם עָנָן֙ וַעֲרָפֶ֔ל כְּשַׁ֖חַר פָּרֻ֣שׂ עַל־הֶהָרִ֑ים עַ֚ם רַ֣ב וְעָצ֔וּם כָּמֹ֗הוּ לֹ֤א נִֽהְיָה֙ מִן־הָ֣עוֹלָ֔ם וְאַֽחֲרָיו֙ לֹ֣א יוֹסֵ֔ף עַד־שְׁנֵ֖י דּ֥וֹר וָדֽוֹר
verses.translation: A day of darkness and gloom,A day of densest cloudSpread like soot over the hills.A vast, enormous horde—Nothing like it has ever happened,And it shall never happen againThrough the years and ages.
verses.rashi: like the dawn, spread over the mountains—The increasing locusts and the shearing locusts are spread over the mountains as the dawn is spread over the entire world. the like of which has never been—that all these species should come one after the other, but the species of arbeh alone did not equal that of Egypt, concerning which it is stated: (Exodus 10:14) “And after it there shall not be so.”

verses.hebrew_full: לְפָנָיו֙ אָ֣כְלָה אֵ֔שׁ וְאַחֲרָ֖יו תְּלַהֵ֣ט לֶהָבָ֑ה כְּגַן־עֵ֨דֶן הָאָ֜רֶץ לְפָנָ֗יו וְאַֽחֲרָיו֙ מִדְבַּ֣ר שְׁמָמָ֔ה וְגַם־פְּלֵיטָ֖ה לֹא־הָ֥יְתָה לּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Their vanguard is a consuming fire,Their rear guard a devouring flame.Before them the land was like the Garden of Eden,Behind them, a desolate waste:Nothing has escaped them.
verses.rashi: Fire consumes before it—They destroy everything as though a fire consumes before them and a flame blazes after them. like the Garden of Eden—was the land before it came, and, after it goes, it leaves the land like a desert wasteland.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּמַרְאֵ֥ה סוּסִ֖ים מַרְאֵ֑הוּ וּכְפָרָשִׁ֖ים כֵּ֥ן יְרוּצֽוּן
verses.translation: They have the appearance of horses,They gallop just like steeds.
verses.rashi: Like the appearance of horses is its appearance—I.e., in their running.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּק֣וֹל מַרְכָּב֗וֹת עַל־רָאשֵׁ֤י הֶהָרִים֙ יְרַקֵּד֔וּן כְּקוֹל֙ לַ֣הַב אֵ֔שׁ אֹכְלָ֖ה קָ֑שׁ כְּעַ֣ם עָצ֔וּם עֱר֖וּךְ מִלְחָמָֽה
verses.translation: With a clatter as of chariotsThey bound on the hilltops,With a noise like a blazing fireConsuming straw;Like an enormous hordeArrayed for battle.
verses.rashi: like the sound of a flame—A flame, consuming stubble, makes noise. arrayed for battle Heb. עֱרוּךְ. Since it is in the construct state, it is vowelized with a “hataf segol.”

verses.hebrew_full: מִפָּנָ֖יו יָחִ֣ילוּ עַמִּ֑ים כׇּל־פָּנִ֖ים קִבְּצ֥וּ פָארֽוּר
verses.translation: Peoples tremble before them,All faces turn ashen.
verses.rashi: gather blackness Heb. קִבְּצוּ פָּארוּר. Blackness like a pot, Cf. (Num. 11:8) “And they cooked in a pot (בַּפָּרוּר).” The “aleph” is superfluous, and it is not read.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּגִבּוֹרִ֣ים יְרֻצ֔וּן כְּאַנְשֵׁ֥י מִלְחָמָ֖ה יַעֲל֣וּ חוֹמָ֑ה וְאִ֤ישׁ בִּדְרָכָיו֙ יֵֽלֵכ֔וּן וְלֹ֥א יְעַבְּט֖וּן אֹרְחוֹתָֽם
verses.translation: They rush like warriors,They scale a wall like fighters.And each keeps to its own track.Their paths never cross;
verses.rashi: and they do not make... crooked Heb. וְעַבְּטוּן. This has no comparison, but its interpretation according to its context is: they do not make crooked.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאִ֤ישׁ אָחִיו֙ לֹ֣א יִדְחָק֔וּן גֶּ֥בֶר בִּמְסִלָּת֖וֹ יֵלֵכ֑וּן וּבְעַ֥ד הַשֶּׁ֛לַח יִפֹּ֖לוּ לֹ֥א יִבְצָֽעוּ
verses.translation: No one jostles another,Each keeps to its own course.And should they fall through a loophole,They do not get hurt.
verses.rashi: and upon the swords Heb. וּבְעַד הַשֶּׁלַח, (verse 9) “through the windows (בְּעַד הַחַלוֹנִים).” Upon the weapons they fall and camp. they do not receive monetary gain Heb. לֹא יִבְצָעוּ. They do not receive monetary gain. And Jonathan interpreted it (הַשֶּׁלַח) as an expression of an errand. Because of the errand of the Holy One, blessed be He, they cast themselves down, and they do not receive monetary gain.

verses.hebrew_full: בָּעִ֣יר יָשֹׁ֗קּוּ בַּֽחוֹמָה֙ יְרֻצ֔וּן בַּבָּתִּ֖ים יַעֲל֑וּ בְּעַ֧ד הַחַלּוֹנִ֛ים יָבֹ֖אוּ כַּגַּנָּֽב
verses.translation: They rush up the wall,They dash about in the city;They climb into the houses,They enter like thievesBy way of the windows.
verses.rashi: In the city they clatter Heb. יָשֹׁקּוּ. This is an expression of making a din. Cf. (Isa. 33:4) “like the roaring (כְּמַשַּׁק) of the cisterns”; (Prov. 28: 15) “A growling (שּׁוֹקֵק) bear”; (Zeph. 2:9) “the clattering of (מִמְשַׁק) the thorns.” Jonathan, however, renders it as an expression of arms (נֶשֶק). They are armed.

verses.hebrew_full: לְפָנָיו֙ רָ֣גְזָה אֶ֔רֶץ רָעֲשׁ֖וּ שָׁמָ֑יִם שֶׁ֤מֶשׁ וְיָרֵ֙חַ֙ קָדָ֔רוּ וְכוֹכָבִ֖ים אָסְפ֥וּ נׇגְהָֽם
verses.translation: Before them earth trembles,Heaven shakes,Sun and moon are darkened,And stars withdraw their brightness.
verses.rashi: the heavens tremble—Because of the retribution coming upon Israel, they quake and tremble. withdraw their shining—Take in their light.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיהֹוָ֗ה נָתַ֤ן קוֹלוֹ֙ לִפְנֵ֣י חֵיל֔וֹ כִּ֣י רַ֤ב מְאֹד֙ מַחֲנֵ֔הוּ כִּ֥י עָצ֖וּם עֹשֵׂ֣ה דְבָר֑וֹ כִּֽי־גָד֧וֹל יוֹם־יְהֹוָ֛ה וְנוֹרָ֥א מְאֹ֖ד וּמִ֥י יְכִילֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: And GOD roars aloudAt the head of the divine army;For vast indeed is this host,Numberless are those that do God’s bidding.For great is the day of GOD,Most terrible—who can endure it?
verses.rashi: And the Lord gave forth (upon them) His voice—by the statement of His prophets prior to His sending this host of His. for His camp is great—I.e, the camp He will send against you if you do not improve.

verses.hebrew_full: וְגַם־עַתָּה֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה שֻׁ֥בוּ עָדַ֖י בְּכׇל־לְבַבְכֶ֑ם וּבְצ֥וֹם וּבִבְכִ֖י וּבְמִסְפֵּֽד
verses.translation: “Yet even now”—says GOD—“Turn back to Me with all your hearts,And with fasting, weeping, and lamenting.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְקִרְע֤וּ לְבַבְכֶם֙ וְאַל־בִּגְדֵיכֶ֔ם וְשׁ֖וּבוּ אֶל־יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֵיכֶ֑ם כִּֽי־חַנּ֤וּן וְרַחוּם֙ ה֔וּא אֶ֤רֶךְ אַפַּ֙יִם֙ וְרַב־חֶ֔סֶד וְנִחָ֖ם עַל־הָרָעָֽה
verses.translation: Rend your heartsRather than your garments,And turn back to the ETERNAL your God.For [God] is gracious and compassionate,Slow to anger, abounding in kindness,And renouncing punishment.
verses.rashi: and not your garments—for I do not pay heed to the rending of your garments. Another explanation: Rend your hearts and you will not need to rend your garments because of mourning. and He repents of the evil—I.e., He turns to another thought.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֥י יוֹדֵ֖עַ יָשׁ֣וּב וְנִחָ֑ם וְהִשְׁאִ֤יר אַחֲרָיו֙ בְּרָכָ֔ה מִנְחָ֣ה וָנֶ֔סֶךְ לַיהֹוָ֖ה אֱלֹהֵיכֶֽם
verses.translation: Who knows but [God] may turn and relent,And leave a blessing behindFor grain offering and drink offeringTo the ETERNAL your God?
verses.rashi: Whoever knows—that he is guilty of iniquity. shall return and repent, and it shall leave—I.e., the hasil and the gazam will leave a blessing after it; through the repentance, a blessing will come in the produce.

verses.hebrew_full: תִּקְע֥וּ שׁוֹפָ֖ר בְּצִיּ֑וֹן קַדְּשׁוּ־צ֖וֹם קִרְא֥וּ עֲצָרָֽה
verses.translation: Blow a horn in Zion,Solemnize a fast,Proclaim an assembly!

verses.hebrew_full: אִסְפוּ־עָ֞ם קַדְּשׁ֤וּ קָהָל֙ קִבְצ֣וּ זְקֵנִ֔ים אִסְפוּ֙ עֽוֹלָלִ֔ים וְיֹנְקֵ֖י שָׁדָ֑יִם יֵצֵ֤א חָתָן֙ מֵֽחֶדְר֔וֹ וְכַלָּ֖ה מֵחֻפָּתָֽהּ
verses.translation: Gather the people,Bid the congregation purify themselves. Bring together the old,Gather the babesAnd the sucklings at the breast;Let the bridegroom come out of his chamber,The bride from her canopied couch.
verses.rashi: assemble the elders Heb. קִבְצוּ. This is of the form of (I Kings 18:19) “Send and gather (קְבֹץ) for me,” an expression which is weak and light.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֤ין הָאוּלָם֙ וְלַמִּזְבֵּ֔חַ יִבְכּוּ֙ הַכֹּ֣הֲנִ֔ים מְשָׁרְתֵ֖י יְהֹוָ֑ה וְֽיֹאמְר֞וּ ח֧וּסָה יְהֹוָ֣ה עַל־עַמֶּ֗ךָ וְאַל־תִּתֵּ֨ן נַחֲלָתְךָ֤ לְחֶרְפָּה֙ לִמְשׇׁל־בָּ֣ם גּוֹיִ֔ם לָ֚מָּה יֹאמְר֣וּ בָעַמִּ֔ים אַיֵּ֖ה אֱלֹהֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: Between the portico and the altar,Let the priests, GOD’s ministers, weepAnd say:“Oh, spare Your people, ETERNAL One!Let not Your possession become a mockery,To be taunted by nations!Let not the peoples say,‘Where is their God?’”
verses.rashi: a derision Heb. לְחֶרְפָּה for nations to make them an example Heb. לְמְשָׁל, an expression of, (Deut. 28:37) “for an example (מָשָל) and for a conversation piece.”

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְקַנֵּ֥א יְהֹוָ֖ה לְאַרְצ֑וֹ וַיַּחְמֹ֖ל עַל־עַמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Then GOD was rousedIn behalf of this landAnd had compassionUpon this people.
verses.rashi: And the Lord was zealous for His land Heb. וַיְקַנֵּא, an expression similar to (Num. 11:29) “Are you zealous (הַמְקַנֵּא) for me?” Their distress entered His heart, and He fought their battle and engaged in their necessities. Our Sages explain it as an expression of warning. He warned the locusts concerning His land. [from Sotah 3a]

verses.hebrew_full: וַיַּ֨עַן יְהֹוָ֜ה וַיֹּ֣אמֶר לְעַמּ֗וֹ הִנְנִ֨י שֹׁלֵ֤חַ לָכֶם֙ אֶת־הַדָּגָן֙ וְהַתִּיר֣וֹשׁ וְהַיִּצְהָ֔ר וּשְׂבַעְתֶּ֖ם אֹת֑וֹ וְלֹא־אֶתֵּ֨ן אֶתְכֶ֥ם ע֛וֹד חֶרְפָּ֖ה בַּגּוֹיִֽם
verses.translation: In response to this covenanted people GOD declared:“I will grant you the new grain,The new wine, and the new oil,And you shall have them in abundance.Nevermore will I let you beA mockery among the nations.
verses.rashi: derision Heb. חֶרְפָּה, a derision, that they will call you ill-provided.

words.eng: inflected m. pers. pron. meaning ‘to you’.
sign of the definite direct object·wheat
sign of the definite direct object
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽאֶת־הַצְּפוֹנִ֞י אַרְחִ֣יק מֵעֲלֵיכֶ֗ם וְהִדַּחְתִּיו֮ אֶל־אֶ֣רֶץ צִיָּ֣ה וּשְׁמָמָה֒ אֶת־פָּנָ֗יו אֶל־הַיָּם֙ הַקַּדְמֹנִ֔י וְסֹפ֖וֹ אֶל־הַיָּ֣ם הָאַחֲר֑וֹן וְעָלָ֣ה בׇאְשׁ֗וֹ וְתַ֙עַל֙ צַחֲנָת֔וֹ כִּ֥י הִגְדִּ֖יל לַעֲשֽׂוֹת
verses.translation: I will drive the northerner far from you,I will thrust it into a parched and desolate land—Its van to the Eastern Sea And its rear to the Western Sea; And the stench of it shall go up,And the foul smell rise.”For [GOD] shall work great deeds.
verses.rashi: And the northerner Heb. הַצְּפוֹנִי. This can be interpreted as referring to the host of locusts, upon which the expression, “and I will drive him to a land barren and desolate,” fits aptly. Another explanation: The people that come from the north, viz., the kings of Assyria. And our Sages (Sukkah 52a) state: This is the temptation, which is hidden (צָפוּן) in a person’s heart. the western sea the eastern sea. And our Sages, who interpreted it [i.e., הַצְּפוֹנִי] as a reference to temptation, interpreted these two seas as the First Temple and the Second Temple. I.e, they explain הַקַּדְמוֹנִי as first and הָאַחֲרוֹן as last. And so they explained it: Because he directed his face toward the First Temple and the Second Temple and destroyed them. [God says: I will drive him out to a land barren and desolate, where he will find no one to incite. That will be because he set his sights for the First Temple and for the Second Temple and, because he incited the people to sin, they were destroyed. The two Temples, where everyone would gather during the three Pilgrimage Festivals, are symbolized by the sea, where water of the streams gather.] And, according to the Targum, who explains it as a reference to the king of Assyria, we must explain “his face to the eastern sea, and his end to the western sea” to mean that part of his army I will send to the east and part of it to the west. its ill savor Heb. צַחֲנָתוֹ. The word בָאְשוֹ, its stench, indicates concerning that it is an expression of filth. for it did great things—I.e, it did a great evil, for it stretched forth its hands upon the great.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·adj
sign of the definite direct object·face

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּֽירְאִ֖י אֲדָמָ֑ה גִּ֣ילִי וּשְׂמָ֔חִי כִּֽי־הִגְדִּ֥יל יְהֹוָ֖ה לַעֲשֽׂוֹת
verses.translation: Fear not, O soil, rejoice and be glad;For GOD has wrought great deeds.
verses.rashi: Have no fear, O land—I.e, the land of Israel, when you repent.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּֽירְאוּ֙ בַּהֲמ֣וֹת שָׂדַ֔י כִּ֥י דָשְׁא֖וּ נְא֣וֹת מִדְבָּ֑ר כִּי־עֵץ֙ נָשָׂ֣א פִרְי֔וֹ תְּאֵנָ֥ה וָגֶ֖פֶן נָתְנ֥וּ חֵילָֽם
verses.translation: Fear not, O beasts of the field,For the pastures in the wildernessAre clothed with grass.The trees have borne their fruit;Fig tree and vineHave yielded their strength.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֣י צִיּ֗וֹן גִּ֤ילוּ וְשִׂמְחוּ֙ בַּיהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֵיכֶ֔ם כִּֽי־נָתַ֥ן לָכֶ֛ם אֶת־הַמּוֹרֶ֖ה לִצְדָקָ֑ה וַיּ֣וֹרֶד לָכֶ֗ם גֶּ֛שֶׁם מוֹרֶ֥ה וּמַלְק֖וֹשׁ בָּרִאשֽׁוֹן
verses.translation: O children of Zion, be glad,Rejoice in the ETERNAL your God.For you have been given the early rain out of kindness,Now the rain is made to fall [as] formerly—The early rain and the late—
verses.rashi: the teacher for justification Heb. מוֹרֶה. Your prophets who teach you to return to Me, in order to justify you. the early rain and the late rain. Heb. מוֹרֶה, like (Deut. 11:14) “the early rain (יוֹרֶה) and the late rain (וּמַלְקוֹֹש).” in the first month—In Nissan. Although the early rain is the first rain, which falls on the seeds, and that is in Marcheshvan, that year they sowed in Nissan, as is explained in Tractate Taanith (5a) that the grain grew in eleven days.
verses.ibn_ezra: And the children: Yefet [ben Ali] said that the instructor (hamoreh) is the prophet that instructs them about the righteous path. And that is why good is coming to them. And it mentioned early rain (moreh) and late rain, as [in] (Judges 15:16), "With the jaw of an ass (chamor), mass (chamor) upon mass"...

words.eng: inflected m. pers. pron. meaning ‘to you’.
sign of the definite direct object·(early) rain
inflected m. pers. pron. meaning ‘to you’.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמָלְא֥וּ הַגֳּרָנ֖וֹת בָּ֑ר וְהֵשִׁ֥יקוּ הַיְקָבִ֖ים תִּיר֥וֹשׁ וְיִצְהָֽר
verses.translation: And threshing floors shall be piled with grain,And vats shall overflow with new wine and oil.
verses.rashi: shall roar Heb. וְהֵשִׁיקוּ, an expression of making noise when the stream runs down from the winepress into the vat (הַיֶקֶב), which is the pit before the winepress

verses.hebrew_full: וְשִׁלַּמְתִּ֤י לָכֶם֙ אֶת־הַשָּׁנִ֔ים אֲשֶׁר֙ אָכַ֣ל הָאַרְבֶּ֔ה הַיֶּ֖לֶק וְהֶחָסִ֣יל וְהַגָּזָ֑ם חֵילִי֙ הַגָּד֔וֹל אֲשֶׁ֥ר שִׁלַּ֖חְתִּי בָּכֶֽם
verses.translation: “I will repay you for the years Consumed by swarms and hoppers,By grubs and locusts,The great army I let loose against you.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace
inflected m. pers. pron. meaning ‘to you’.
sign of the definite direct object·year

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲכַלְתֶּ֤ם אָכוֹל֙ וְשָׂב֔וֹעַ וְהִלַּלְתֶּ֗ם אֶת־שֵׁ֤ם יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹ֣הֵיכֶ֔ם אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֥ה עִמָּכֶ֖ם לְהַפְלִ֑יא וְלֹא־יֵבֹ֥שׁוּ עַמִּ֖י לְעוֹלָֽם
verses.translation: And you shall eat your fillAnd praise the name of the ETERNAL your GodWho dealt so wondrously with you—My people shall be shamed no more.

verses.hebrew_full: וִידַעְתֶּ֗ם כִּ֣י בְקֶ֤רֶב יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ אָ֔נִי וַאֲנִ֛י יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהֵיכֶ֖ם וְאֵ֣ין ע֑וֹד וְלֹא־יֵבֹ֥שׁוּ עַמִּ֖י לְעוֹלָֽם
verses.translation: And you shall knowThat I am in the midst of Israel:That I the ETERNAL One am your GodAnd there is no other.And My people shall be shamed no more.”

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)
I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: וְהָיָ֣ה אַחֲרֵי־כֵ֗ן אֶשְׁפּ֤וֹךְ אֶת־רוּחִי֙ עַל־כׇּל־בָּשָׂ֔ר וְנִבְּא֖וּ בְּנֵיכֶ֣ם וּבְנוֹתֵיכֶ֑ם זִקְנֵיכֶם֙ חֲלֹמ֣וֹת יַחֲלֹמ֔וּן בַּח֣וּרֵיכֶ֔ם חֶזְיֹנ֖וֹת יִרְאֽוּ
verses.translation: After that,I will pour out My spirit on all flesh;Your sons and daughters shall prophesy;Your elders shall dream dreams,And your youths shall see visions.
verses.rashi: And it shall come to pass afterwards—in the future. upon all flesh—I.e, upon anyone whose heart becomes soft as flesh. Comp. (Ezekiel 36:26) “And I will give you a heart of flesh.”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to smell

verses.hebrew_full: וְגַ֥ם עַל־הָעֲבָדִ֖ים וְעַל־הַשְּׁפָח֑וֹת בַּיָּמִ֣ים הָהֵ֔מָּה אֶשְׁפּ֖וֹךְ אֶת־רוּחִֽי
verses.translation: I will even pour out My spiritUpon male and female slaves in those days.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to smell

verses.hebrew_full: וְנָֽתַתִּי֙ מֽוֹפְתִ֔ים בַּשָּׁמַ֖יִם וּבָאָ֑רֶץ דָּ֣ם וָאֵ֔שׁ וְתִֽימְר֖וֹת עָשָֽׁן
verses.translation: Before the great and terrible day of GOD comes, I will set portents in the sky and on earth:Blood and fire and pillars of smoke;
verses.rashi: and pillars of smoke Heb. וְתִימְרוֹת. Perpendicular pillars of smoke, as tall as a palm tree.

verses.hebrew_full: הַשֶּׁ֙מֶשׁ֙ יֵהָפֵ֣ךְ לְחֹ֔שֶׁךְ וְהַיָּרֵ֖חַ לְדָ֑ם לִפְנֵ֗י בּ֚וֹא י֣וֹם יְהֹוָ֔ה הַגָּד֖וֹל וְהַנּוֹרָֽא
verses.translation: The sun shall turn into darknessAnd the moon into blood.
verses.rashi: shall turn to darkness—to embarrass those who prostrate themselves to the sun.

verses.hebrew_full: וְהָיָ֗ה כֹּ֧ל אֲשֶׁר־יִקְרָ֛א בְּשֵׁ֥ם יְהֹוָ֖ה יִמָּלֵ֑ט כִּ֠י בְּהַר־צִיּ֨וֹן וּבִירוּשָׁלִַ֜ם תִּהְיֶ֣ה פְלֵיטָ֗ה כַּֽאֲשֶׁר֙ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה וּבַ֨שְּׂרִידִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה קֹרֵֽא
verses.translation: But everyone who invokes GOD’s name shall escape; for there shall be a remnant on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, as GOD promised. Anyone who invokes GOD will be among the survivors.
verses.rashi: And it shall come to pass that whoever shall call etc. as... said—And where did He say it? (Deut. 28:10), “And all the peoples of the earth shall see that the Name of the Lord is called on you.” [from Mechilta, Ex. 12:25] and among the survivors—And in the remnant that will remain. whom the Lord invites Heb. קֹרֵא, an expression of those invited to a banquet or to the service of the king, an expression of invitation.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֗י הִנֵּ֛ה בַּיָּמִ֥ים הָהֵ֖מָּה וּבָעֵ֣ת הַהִ֑יא אֲשֶׁ֥ר (אשוב) אָשִׁ֛יב אֶת־שְׁב֥וּת יְהוּדָ֖ה וִירוּשָׁלִָֽם
verses.translation: For lo! in those daysAnd in that time,When I restore the fortunesOf Judah and Jerusalem,

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·captivity

verses.hebrew_full: וְקִבַּצְתִּי֙ אֶת־כׇּל־הַגּוֹיִ֔ם וְה֣וֹרַדְתִּ֔ים אֶל־עֵ֖מֶק יְהוֹשָׁפָ֑ט וְנִשְׁפַּטְתִּ֨י עִמָּ֜ם שָׁ֗ם עַל־עַמִּ֨י וְנַחֲלָתִ֤י יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר פִּזְּר֣וּ בַגּוֹיִ֔ם וְאֶת־אַרְצִ֖י חִלֵּֽקוּ
verses.translation: I will gather all the nationsAnd bring them down to the Valley of Jehoshaphat. There I will contend with themOver My very own people, Israel,Which they scattered among the nations.For they divided My land among themselves
verses.rashi: to the Valley of Jehoshaphat—I will descend with them to the depth of justice. Jehoshaphat—The judgments of the Lord.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·all·n m
sign of the definite direct object·land

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֶל־עַמִּ֖י יַדּ֣וּ גוֹרָ֑ל וַֽיִּתְּנ֤וּ הַיֶּ֙לֶד֙ בַּזּוֹנָ֔ה וְהַיַּלְדָּ֛ה מָכְר֥וּ בַיַּ֖יִן וַיִּשְׁתּֽוּ
verses.translation: And cast lots over My people;And they bartered a boy for a whore,And sold a girl for wine, which they drank.
verses.rashi: they cast lots Heb. יִדּוּ. Like (Lam. 3:53) “And they cast (וַיַּדּוּ) a stone upon me.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֠גַ֠ם מָה־אַתֶּ֥ם לִי֙ צֹ֣ר וְצִיד֔וֹן וְכֹ֖ל גְּלִיל֣וֹת פְּלָ֑שֶׁת הַגְּמ֗וּל אַתֶּם֙ מְשַׁלְּמִ֣ים עָלָ֔י וְאִם־גֹּמְלִ֤ים אַתֶּם֙ עָלַ֔י קַ֣ל מְהֵרָ֔ה אָשִׁ֥יב גְּמֻֽלְכֶ֖ם בְּרֹאשְׁכֶֽם
verses.translation: What is this you are doing to Me, O Tyre, Sidon, and all the districts of Philistia? Are you requiting Me for something I have done, or are you doing something for My benefit? Quick as a flash, I will pay you back;
verses.rashi: Are you paying Me recompense—Is this the recompense for what Solomon gave Hiram, the king of Tyre, twenty cities (I Kings 9:11)? swiftly Heb. קַל מְהֵרָה, an expression of eagerness.

words.eng: interr pron·sign of the definite direct object
sign of the definite direct object
to be in a covenant of peace
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁר־כַּסְפִּ֥י וּזְהָבִ֖י לְקַחְתֶּ֑ם וּמַֽחֲמַדַּי֙ הַטֹּבִ֔ים הֲבֵאתֶ֖ם לְהֵיכְלֵיכֶֽם
verses.translation: for you have taken My gold and My silver, and have carried off My precious treasures to your palaces;

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְנֵ֤י יְהוּדָה֙ וּבְנֵ֣י יְרוּשָׁלִַ֔ם מְכַרְתֶּ֖ם לִבְנֵ֣י הַיְּוָנִ֑ים לְמַ֥עַן הַרְחִיקָ֖ם מֵעַ֥ל גְּבוּלָֽם
verses.translation: and you have sold the people of Judah and the people of Jerusalem to the Ionians, so that you have removed them far away from their homeland.

verses.hebrew_full: הִנְנִ֣י מְעִירָ֔ם מִ֨ן־הַמָּק֔וֹם אֲשֶׁר־מְכַרְתֶּ֥ם אֹתָ֖ם שָׁ֑מָּה וַהֲשִׁבֹתִ֥י גְמֻֽלְכֶ֖ם בְּרֹאשְׁכֶֽם
verses.translation: Behold, I will rouse them to leave the place you have sold them to, and I will pay you back:

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וּמָכַרְתִּ֞י אֶת־בְּנֵיכֶ֣ם וְאֶת־בְּנֽוֹתֵיכֶ֗ם בְּיַד֙ בְּנֵ֣י יְהוּדָ֔ה וּמְכָר֥וּם לִשְׁבָאיִ֖ם אֶל־גּ֣וֹי רָח֑וֹק כִּ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה דִּבֵּֽר
verses.translation: I will deliver your sons and daughters into the hands of the people of Judah, and they will sell them into captivity to a distant nation—for GOD has spoken.
verses.rashi: to the Shebaites—To the children of Sheba. for the Lord has spoken—Now where did He speak? (Gen. 9:25) “A slave of slaves shall he be to his brothers.” So it is explained in Mechilta (Ex. 12:25).

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·son
sign of the definite direct object·n f

verses.hebrew_full: קִרְאוּ־זֹאת֙ בַּגּוֹיִ֔ם קַדְּשׁ֖וּ מִלְחָמָ֑ה הָעִ֙ירוּ֙ הַגִּבּוֹרִ֔ים יִגְּשׁ֣וּ יַֽעֲל֔וּ כֹּ֖ל אַנְשֵׁ֥י הַמִּלְחָמָֽה
verses.translation: Proclaim this among the nations:Prepare for battle!Arouse the warriors,Let all the fighters come and draw near!
verses.rashi: prepare war—if you can wage war against Me.

verses.hebrew_full: כֹּ֤תּוּ אִתֵּיכֶם֙ לַחֲרָב֔וֹת וּמַזְמְרֹֽתֵיכֶ֖ם לִרְמָחִ֑ים הַֽחַלָּ֔שׁ יֹאמַ֖ר גִּבּ֥וֹר אָֽנִי
verses.translation: Beat your plowshares into swords,And your pruning hooks into spears.Let even the weakling say, “I am strong.”
verses.rashi: your plowshares Heb. אִתֵּיכֶם, koltres in O.F:, colters. and your pruning hooks Heb. וּמַזְמְרוֹתֵיכֶם, serpes in French.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object
I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: ע֣וּשׁוּ וָבֹ֧אוּ כׇֽל־הַגּוֹיִ֛ם מִסָּבִ֖יב וְנִקְבָּ֑צוּ שָׁ֕מָּה הַֽנְחַ֥ת יְהֹוָ֖ה גִּבּוֹרֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Rouse yourselves and come,All you nations;Come togetherFrom round about.There bring down Your warriors, O ETERNAL One!
verses.rashi: Gather Heb. עוּשׁוּ. Like חוּשוּ, hasten, so did Menahem associate it. Jonathan, however, renders: they shall gather, and I say that it is an expression of a block of iron (עֶשֶת), which is gathered together, masse in French, and the “tav” is not a radical. there the Lord shall break your mighty men Heb. הַנְחַת. There in the gathering of the Valley of Jehoshaphat, He will break your might. So did Jonathan render it, but I do not know whether it is an Aramaic expression of lowering (נחת) or an expression of breaking (חתת), and the “nun” is defective. Cf. (Dan. 2:25) “brought Daniel in (הנְעַל) before the king,” which has no radical but על.

verses.hebrew_full: יֵע֙וֹרוּ֙ וְיַעֲל֣וּ הַגּוֹיִ֔ם אֶל־עֵ֖מֶק יְהוֹשָׁפָ֑ט כִּ֣י שָׁ֗ם אֵשֵׁ֛ב לִשְׁפֹּ֥ט אֶת־כׇּל־הַגּוֹיִ֖ם מִסָּבִֽיב
verses.translation: Let the nations rouse themselves and march upTo the Valley of Jehoshaphat; For there I will sit in judgmentOver all the nations round about.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·all·n m

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁלְח֣וּ מַגָּ֔ל כִּ֥י בָשַׁ֖ל קָצִ֑יר בֹּ֤אֽוּ רְדוּ֙ כִּֽי־מָ֣לְאָה גַּ֔ת הֵשִׁ֙יקוּ֙ הַיְקָבִ֔ים כִּ֥י רַבָּ֖ה רָעָתָֽם
verses.translation: Swing the sickle,For the crop is ripe;Come and tread,For the winepress is full,The vats are overflowing!For great is their wickedness.
verses.rashi: Stretch out a sickle etc.—Draw the swords, you who go on My mission to destroy the nations. for the harvest is ripe—I.e, for their time has arrived, and he compared the matter to harvest, which is harvested with sickles when it becomes completely ripe. come press for the winepress is full—of grapes. Come and tread them; i.e., the measure of their iniquity is full. press Heb. רְדוּ, an expression of pressing [or ruling]. Cf. (Gen. 1:28) “And rule (וּרְדוּ) over the fish of the sea.” and the vats shall roar—The sound of the stream of the wine going down in the vats before the winepresses is heard.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲמוֹנִ֣ים הֲמוֹנִ֔ים בְּעֵ֖מֶק הֶחָר֑וּץ כִּ֤י קָרוֹב֙ י֣וֹם יְהֹוָ֔ה בְּעֵ֖מֶק הֶחָרֽוּץ
verses.translation: Multitudes upon multitudesIn the Valley of Decision!For the day of GOD is at handIn the Valley of Decision.
verses.rashi: in the valley of decision—For there it is decided that the judgment is true. And so did Jonathan render: the valley of judgment.

verses.hebrew_full: שֶׁ֥מֶשׁ וְיָרֵ֖חַ קָדָ֑רוּ וְכוֹכָבִ֖ים אָסְפ֥וּ נׇגְהָֽם
verses.translation: Sun and moon are darkened,And stars withdraw their brightness.
verses.rashi: have withdrawn their shining—Have taken in their shining.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיהֹוָ֞ה מִצִּיּ֣וֹן יִשְׁאָ֗ג וּמִירוּשָׁלִַ֙ם֙ יִתֵּ֣ן קוֹל֔וֹ וְרָעֲשׁ֖וּ שָׁמַ֣יִם וָאָ֑רֶץ וַֽיהֹוָה֙ מַחֲסֶ֣ה לְעַמּ֔וֹ וּמָע֖וֹז לִבְנֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל
verses.translation: And GOD will roar from Zion,And shout aloud from Jerusalem,So that heaven and earth tremble.But GOD will be a shelter to this covenanted people,A refuge to the children of Israel.
verses.rashi: shall roar from Zion—Because of what they did to Zion. and the heavens and earth shall quake—He will deal retribution upon the heavenly princes and then upon the nations. a shelter Heb. מַחֲסֶה, an expression of a cover, abrier in O.F., abri in modern French, a shelter.

verses.hebrew_full: וִידַעְתֶּ֗ם כִּ֣י אֲנִ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹ֣הֵיכֶ֔ם שֹׁכֵ֖ן בְּצִיּ֣וֹן הַר־קׇדְשִׁ֑י וְהָיְתָ֤ה יְרוּשָׁלִַ֙ם֙ קֹ֔דֶשׁ וְזָרִ֥ים לֹא־יַֽעַבְרוּ־בָ֖הּ עֽוֹד
verses.translation: And you shall know that I the ETERNAL your GodDwell in Zion, My holy mount.And Jerusalem shall be holy;Nevermore shall strangers pass through it.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)
not·to pass over or by or through·meaning ‘in her’

verses.hebrew_full: וְהָיָה֩ בַיּ֨וֹם הַה֜וּא יִטְּפ֧וּ הֶהָרִ֣ים עָסִ֗יס וְהַגְּבָעוֹת֙ תֵּלַ֣כְנָה חָלָ֔ב וְכׇל־אֲפִיקֵ֥י יְהוּדָ֖ה יֵ֣לְכוּ מָ֑יִם וּמַעְיָ֗ן מִבֵּ֤ית יְהֹוָה֙ יֵצֵ֔א וְהִשְׁקָ֖ה אֶת־נַ֥חַל הַשִּׁטִּֽים
verses.translation: And in that day,The mountains shall drip with wine,The hills shall flow with milk,And all the watercourses of Judah shall flow with water;A spring shall issue from the House of GOD And shall water the Wadi of the Acacias.
verses.rashi: springs Heb. אֲפִיקֵי, a place of the source of water. and water the valley of Shittim—According to its apparent meaning, and it will water the valley of Shittim (Targum), and, according to its Midrashic meaning, He will atone for the iniquity of Peor.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·torrent

verses.hebrew_full: מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ לִשְׁמָמָ֣ה תִֽהְיֶ֔ה וֶאֱד֕וֹם לְמִדְבַּ֥ר שְׁמָמָ֖ה תִּֽהְיֶ֑ה מֵֽחֲמַס֙ בְּנֵ֣י יְהוּדָ֔ה אֲשֶׁר־שָׁפְכ֥וּ דָם־נָקִ֖יא בְּאַרְצָֽם
verses.translation: Egypt shall be a desolation,And Edom a desolate waste,Because of the outrage to the people of Judah,In whose land they shed the blood of the innocent.
verses.rashi: Egypt etc. and Edom—He juxtaposed them as regards their retribution. You find that, were it not for Egypt, Edom would not exist, as it is stated: (I Kings 11:16 21) “For Joab and all Israel remained [stationed] there for six months etc. every male in Edom.” And it is stated there: (verse 17) “Adad fled, he and some Edomite men etc. And Hadad pleased Pharaoh very much etc. And Hadad etc. that David slept with his fathers etc.” And, at the end, (verse 25) “And he was an adversary to Solomon (sic).” Said the Holy One, blessed be He: I will destroy both of them. because of the violence done to the children of Judah—Because of the violence they did to the children of Judah, viz., that they shed their blood.

verses.hebrew_full: וִיהוּדָ֖ה לְעוֹלָ֣ם תֵּשֵׁ֑ב וִירוּשָׁלִַ֖ם לְד֥וֹר וָדֽוֹר
verses.translation: But Judah shall be inhabited forever,And Jerusalem throughout the ages.

verses.hebrew_full: וְנִקֵּ֖יתִי דָּמָ֣ם לֹֽא־נִקֵּ֑יתִי וַיהֹוָ֖ה שֹׁכֵ֥ן בְּצִיּֽוֹן
verses.translation: Thus I will treat as innocent their bloodThat I have not treated as innocent; And GOD shall dwell in Zion.
verses.rashi: Now should I cleanse, their blood I will not cleanse—Even if I cleanse them of other sins in their hands, and of the evils they have done to Me, I will not cleanse them of the blood of the children of Judah. When will this come to pass? At the time the Holy One, blessed be He, dwells in Zion.
